title: Software Engineer
description: Designs, builds, and maintains software applications across the stack. Works with teams to ship reliable, scalable code.

title: Frontend Engineer
description: Builds user-facing web interfaces using modern JavaScript frameworks, focusing on performance, accessibility, and visual polish.

title: Backend Engineer
description: Develops server-side logic, databases, and APIs that power applications. Focuses on performance, security, and scalability.

title: Full-Stack Engineer
description: Fluent across the entire web stack — from database to UI. Builds and ships features end-to-end, often in fast-moving startup environments.

title: Mobile Developer
description: Builds native and cross-platform apps for iOS and Android. Balances performance with great user experience on small screens.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: Automates deployment pipelines, manages cloud infrastructure, and bridges the gap between development and operations teams.

title: Cybersecurity Analyst
description: Monitors, detects, and responds to security threats. Conducts vulnerability assessments and protects organizational systems and data.

title: Game Developer
description: Creates interactive game experiences using engines like Unity or Unreal. Combines programming skills with a strong sense for player experience.

title: AR/VR Developer
description: Builds immersive experiences for augmented and virtual reality platforms. Merges spatial computing, 3D design, and interaction design.

title: Data Scientist
description: Extracts insights from large datasets using statistical modeling and machine learning. Turns messy data into decisions that drive strategy.

title: Data Analyst
description: Interprets data to help organizations make better decisions. Builds dashboards, writes reports, and communicates findings clearly to stakeholders.

title: Machine Learning Engineer
description: Designs and deploys ML models at scale. Bridges the gap between research and production systems.

title: Quantitative Analyst
description: Applies advanced mathematics and statistics to financial modeling, risk assessment, and algorithmic trading strategies.

title: Product Manager
description: Leads cross-functional teams to define, build, and launch products. Balances user needs, business goals, and technical constraints.

title: Product Designer
description: Shapes the look, feel, and usability of digital products. Conducts user research and creates wireframes, prototypes, and polished visual designs.

title: UX Researcher
description: Studies how users interact with products through interviews, usability tests, and surveys. Translates behavioral insights into actionable design recommendations.

title: Management Consultant
description: Helps organizations solve complex strategic, operational, and financial problems. Delivers recommendations through data analysis and structured frameworks.

title: Investment Analyst
description: Researches companies and markets to guide investment decisions. Builds financial models and presents recommendations to portfolio managers or clients.

title: Marketing Analyst
description: Measures and interprets marketing campaign performance. Uses data to optimize channels, inform strategy, and justify spend.

title: Content Strategist
description: Plans and oversees content creation across channels to serve brand goals. Combines editorial judgment, SEO knowledge, and audience insight.

title: Social Media Manager
description: Manages brand presence across social platforms. Creates content calendars, drives engagement, and analyzes performance metrics.

title: Video Producer
description: Plans, shoots, and edits video content for brands, media companies, or independent projects. Manages the entire production pipeline.

title: Graphic Designer
description: Creates visual assets for digital and print media — logos, marketing materials, brand systems, and editorial layouts.

title: Public Health Analyst
description: Analyzes health data and policies to understand disease patterns and guide public health interventions at community or national scale.

title: Policy Analyst
description: Evaluates existing policies and develops evidence-based recommendations for government agencies, think tanks, or nonprofits.

title: Nonprofit Program Associate
description: Supports the design and execution of community programs at nonprofits. Coordinates volunteers, manages logistics, and tracks program outcomes.

title: Entrepreneur / Startup Founder
description: Builds a company from the ground up. Identifies market opportunities, assembles teams, raises funding, and navigates the chaos of early-stage growth.

title: Technical Writer
description: Translates complex technical concepts into clear, accessible documentation — from API references to user guides and onboarding materials.

title: Project Manager
description: Plans, tracks, and delivers complex projects on time and on budget. Coordinates teams, manages risks, and keeps stakeholders aligned.

title: Robotics Engineer
description: Designs and programs robots and autonomous systems. Works at the intersection of mechanical, electrical, and software engineering.

title: Journalist / Reporter
description: Investigates stories, interviews sources, and writes news or feature articles for print, digital, broadcast, or podcast outlets.

title: Editor (Book / Magazine / Digital)
description: Shapes written content for clarity, accuracy, and style. Works with writers to develop ideas and ensure quality across publications.

title: Copywriter
description: Writes persuasive, on-brand copy for ads, websites, emails, and campaigns. Balances creativity with strategy to move audiences to action.

title: Creative Writer / Author
description: Crafts fiction, poetry, essays, or screenplays. Pursues publishing through literary journals, agents, presses, or independent platforms.

title: Screenwriter
description: Develops scripts for film, TV, or streaming. Constructs compelling characters, dialogue, and narrative structure for visual storytelling.

title: Documentary Filmmaker
description: Researches and produces non-fiction films about real events, people, and issues. Combines investigative instincts with cinematic craft.

title: Museum Curator
description: Acquires, organizes, and interprets collections for museums and galleries. Develops exhibitions that educate and engage the public.

title: Archivist / Digital Preservation Specialist
description: Organizes, preserves, and provides access to historical records and cultural materials — increasingly in digital formats.

title: Historian / Academic Researcher
description: Conducts original historical research, publishes scholarship, and teaches. Works in universities, research institutes, or public history settings.

title: Philosopher / Ethics Researcher
description: Investigates foundational questions in ethics, epistemology, logic, and metaphysics through argument and analysis. Relevant in academia, AI ethics, law, and policy.

title: Sociologist / Social Researcher
description: Studies social structures, inequality, and human behavior through quantitative and qualitative research. Works in academia, government, think tanks, or NGOs.

title: Research Psychologist
description: Designs and conducts empirical studies on behavior and cognition. Works in academic labs, hospitals, or private research organizations.

title: Clinical Psychologist / Counselor
description: Assesses and treats mental health conditions through evidence-based therapy. Requires licensure; works in hospitals, private practice, or community settings.

title: International Relations Specialist
description: Analyzes global political dynamics and foreign policy for governments, NGOs, international organizations, or think tanks.

title: Lawyer (Pre-Law / Legal Track)
description: Represents clients, drafts legal documents, and advises on rights and obligations. Requires law school; paths include litigation, corporate law, public interest, and policy.

title: Foreign Service Officer / Diplomat
description: Represents national interests abroad through negotiations, reporting, and relationship-building. Works for the State Department or international organizations.

title: K-12 Teacher / Educator
description: Designs and delivers lessons for elementary, middle, or high school students. Combines subject expertise with classroom management and a passion for student development.

title: Higher Education Administrator
description: Manages programs, student services, or operations at colleges and universities. Roles include admissions, student affairs, academic advising, and institutional research.

title: Public Relations Specialist
description: Manages the public image of organizations and individuals through media outreach, press releases, crisis communication, and event planning.

title: Urban / City Planner
description: Shapes the development of cities and communities by analyzing land use, transportation, housing, and environmental impact.

title: Environmental Consultant
description: Advises organizations on environmental regulations, sustainability strategy, and impact assessments. Works across corporate, government, and NGO sectors.

title: Social Worker / Case Manager
description: Supports individuals and families navigating poverty, mental health crises, housing instability, and other challenges. Requires licensure for clinical roles.

title: Human Rights Advocate / Activist
description: Works to protect civil and human rights through legal advocacy, organizing, documentation, and policy reform at NGOs, legal clinics, or international bodies.

title: Librarian / Information Specialist
description: Organizes and provides access to information resources. Modern roles often involve digital literacy, data management, instructional support, and community programming.

title: Translator / Interpreter
description: Converts written or spoken content between languages. Works in legal, medical, literary, diplomatic, or corporate settings.

title: Podcast Producer / Audio Journalist
description: Develops, records, edits, and distributes audio content. Combines storytelling instincts, technical audio skills, and an understanding of audience.

title: Art Director
description: Oversees the visual style and creative direction of campaigns, publications, or digital products. Leads design teams and maintains brand coherence.

title: Fashion Designer / Textile Artist
description: Creates clothing, accessories, or textiles. Works at the intersection of art, craft, and commerce, from high fashion to sustainable apparel.

title: Architect (Entry Level)
description: Designs buildings and spaces that balance aesthetics, function, safety, and sustainability. Requires licensure; entry roles involve drafting, modeling, and supporting senior architects.

title: Performing Artist / Theater Professional
description: Acts, directs, designs, or produces for stage, screen, or live performance. Paths include acting, stage management, scenic design, and arts administration.

title: Musician / Music Producer
description: Creates, performs, or produces music professionally. Roles span live performance, studio production, composition, and music direction for media.

title: Data Journalist
description: Combines data analysis and storytelling to produce news stories. Uses statistics, visualization, and investigative reporting to uncover and communicate findings.

title: Digital Humanities Specialist
description: Applies computational methods — text analysis, mapping, data visualization — to humanities research. Works in academia, libraries, archives, or cultural institutions.

title: Community Organizer
description: Builds power in communities by recruiting members, running campaigns, and advocating for systemic change. Works for unions, political campaigns, or grassroots organizations.

title: Epidemiologist
description: Studies the distribution and causes of disease in populations. Designs surveillance systems, analyzes outbreak data, and informs public health interventions.

title: Finance Analyst
description: Prepares financial reports, models forecasts, and supports budgeting and strategic planning at corporations or financial institutions.

title: Biomedical Researcher
description: Conducts laboratory and clinical research to advance understanding of disease and develop new treatments. Works in academic labs, hospitals, or biotech companies.

title: Science Communicator / Educator
description: Makes complex science accessible to general audiences through writing, video, teaching, or public outreach. Works in media, museums, nonprofits, or research institutions.

title: Financial Planner / Advisor
description: Helps individuals and families manage investments, plan for retirement, and navigate financial decisions. Often requires CFP certification.

title: Recruiter (Entry Level)
description: Sources, screens, and hires candidates for organizations. Builds relationships with job seekers and hiring managers to fill roles quickly and well.

title: Customer Success Manager
description: Ensures clients get value from a product or service. Builds long-term relationships, reduces churn, and serves as the voice of the customer inside the company.

title: Event Planner / Coordinator
description: Organizes conferences, corporate events, galas, and experiential activations. Manages vendors, logistics, budgets, and timelines simultaneously.

title: Teaching Assistant / Academic Tutor
description: Supports student learning in academic settings. Leads recitations, grades work, holds office hours, and assists faculty in undergraduate or graduate courses.

title: Communications Coordinator
description: Supports an organization’s messaging and outreach by writing content, coordinating campaigns, and aligning stakeholders across projects.

title: Public Relations Associate
description: Helps shape public perception through messaging, media outreach, relationship building, and communication strategy.

title: Academic Advisor (Entry)
description: Guides students in academic planning, goal setting, and navigating resources, while coordinating support across campus.

title: Human Resources Coordinator
description: Supports hiring and people operations by coordinating processes, communication, and employee experience across teams.

title: Nonprofit Program Assistant
description: Helps run community or nonprofit programs by coordinating logistics, communication, and day-to-day operations.

title: Fundraising / Development Associate
description: Supports fundraising efforts through donor communication, event coordination, and relationship-building activities.

title: Patient Advocate
description: Helps patients navigate services and resources by resolving issues, coordinating support, and communicating clearly with stakeholders.

title: Admissions Counselor (Entry)
description: Supports recruitment and advising through presentations, student outreach, and coordination of admissions activities.

title: Creative Producer
description: Coordinates creative projects end-to-end by managing timelines, stakeholders, and story-driven content production.

title: Academic Program Assistant
description: Supports academic programs through scheduling, coordination, communication, and administrative organization.

title: Communications Assistant
description: Assists with writing, coordination, and content distribution to support communications and outreach efforts.

title: Editorial Assistant
description: Supports editorial workflows by editing, organizing drafts, and ensuring quality, clarity, and consistency in written content.

title: People Operations Assistant
description: Supports people-focused processes such as onboarding, coordination, and internal communication to improve employee experience.

title: Recruiting Coordinator
description: Coordinates recruiting logistics by scheduling, communicating with candidates, and supporting hiring processes end-to-end.

title: Community Outreach Coordinator
description: Builds partnerships and runs outreach initiatives through communication, relationship building, and event coordination.

title: Advocacy Program Assistant
description: Supports advocacy initiatives through research, writing, coordination, and clear communication with communities and partners.

title: Research Assistant (Social Sciences)
description: Supports social science research through organizing studies, assisting with methods, and communicating findings clearly.

title: Behavioral Health Program Assistant
description: Supports behavioral health or wellness programs through communication, organization, and coordination with care resources.

title: Events Coordinator
description: Plans and executes events by managing logistics, communication, timelines, and cross-team coordination.

title: Arts Program Assistant
description: Supports arts or creative programs by coordinating operations, communications, and event-related logistics.

title: Operations Assistant
description: Supports day-to-day operations by keeping processes organized, communicating clearly, and solving logistical problems.

title: Client Services Associate
description: Supports clients by communicating clearly, building relationships, and resolving issues to improve customer outcomes.

title: Dancer / Performer
description: Performs choreographed or improvised dance pieces in live or recorded settings, collaborating with choreographers and other performers.

title: Singer / Vocal Performer
description: Uses vocal technique and musical expression to perform solo or in ensembles across live, studio, or recorded environments.

title: Actor / Theatre Performer
description: Portrays characters through performance, voice, and movement in theatrical, film, or live productions.

title: Musician / Instrumentalist
description: Plays musical instruments in solo or ensemble settings, developing technical proficiency and musical interpretation.

title: Content Creator / Performer
description: Creates and performs engaging content for digital platforms, combining creativity, performance, and audience engagement.

title: Publishing / Editorial Assistant
description: Supports book or magazine publishing through manuscript evaluation, copyediting, fact-checking, author communication, and production coordination.
